phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest Fed and Bank of Japan (BOJ) monetary policy data. Fetch and report: - Fed current policy stance (Hawkish / Neutral / Dovish) and next meeting rate-cut probability (from CME FedWatch — exact %) - Fed Funds Rate current level (exact %) and year-end terminal rate expectation (from CME FedWatch year-end strip or analyst consensus — exact %) - BOJ current policy stance — has the BOJ officially exited its Negative Interest Rate Policy (NIRP)? Current policy rate (exact %) - BOJ next meeting rate-hike probability (from OIS pricing or analyst consensus — exact %) - BOJ Year-End Rate Expectation: where is the BOJ policy rate expected to settle by year-end? (from OIS pricing or consensus — exact %) - Yield Curve Control (YCC) status: Is YCC still in place? What is the current 10Y JGB yield cap or reference band, if any? Any recent YCC adjustments or abolishment? - Any Japanese Ministry of Finance (MoF) intervention warnings or statements in the last 48 hours — specify the USDJPY level at which MoF officials have issued verbal warnings. Note: MoF intervention risk rises sharply when USDJPY trades above 150.00–155.00 - Any Fed or BOJ speaker comments in the last 48 hours - Which central bank is driving the policy divergence and in which direction ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Neutral/Dovish] | Current Rate: [X%] | Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - BOJ Stance: [Hawkish/Neutral/Dovish] | Current Rate: [X%] | Hike Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Rate Target: [X%] - NIRP Exit: [Confirmed/Not Yet] | YCC Status: [Active/Adjusted/Abolished] | JGB 10Y Cap: [level or "None"] - Rate Differential by Year-End: [Fed Terminal – BOJ Target = bps gap] — widening or narrowing vs current? - MoF Intervention Risk: [Low/Medium/High/Extreme] | Warning Level: [USDJPY level cited by MoF, or "No recent warning"] - Dominant CB Driver: [Fed/BOJ/Equal] - USDJPY Policy Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - POLICY DIVERGENCE (Fed vs BOJ)] - Fed Outlook: [stance + current rate % + next meeting cut probability % + year-end terminal rate %] - BOJ Outlook: [stance + current rate % + next meeting hike probability % + year-end rate target %] - YCC / NIRP Status: [current status + any recent adjustments] - Year-End Rate Gap: [Fed terminal – BOJ target in bps, widening/narrowing] - MoF Intervention Risk: [level + USDJPY warning threshold cited, or None] - Key Speaker Note: [most recent Fed/BOJ comment in 48h, or None] - Core Logic: [why this divergence + rate gap + BOJ normalization pace matters for USDJPY today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

[Yes/No] - DXY: [level] | Today's Move: [Up/Down X%] | Spread-DXY Correlation: [Confirming / Diverging — explain briefly] - Notable JGB Event: [BOJ operation / auction / yield spike, or None] - Bond Market Bias: [USD Favored / JPY Favored / Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P2 - YIELD DIFFERENTIALS (US Treasuries vs JGBs)] - US 2Y / JP 2Y: [% / %] | Spread: [bps] | Trend: [Widening/Narrowing] - US 10Y / JP 10Y: [% / %] | Spread: [bps] | Trend: [Widening/Narrowing] | Extreme? [Yes/No] - DXY: [level] | Move: [direction %] | Spread-DXY Alignment: [Confirming / Diverging] - Notable JGB Event: [BOJ operation / auction detail / calm] - Core Logic: [what the US-JP 10Y spread move + DXY correlation implies for USDJPY today — widening = bullish USDJPY, narrowing = bearish USDJPY] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): US Data: - CPI (Core + Headline): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - NFP (Non-Farm Payrolls): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - GDP (latest quarterly print): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - Retail Sales: Actual vs Forecast vs Previous Japan Data: - Tokyo CPI (Core): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous — this is a leading indicator for national CPI and a key BOJ input - Shunto Wage Growth (Spring Labor Negotiations): Latest available result — state exact wage growth % agreed. This is the single most important domestic indicator for BOJ rate hike decisions. If Shunto data was released this cycle, flag its impact on BOJ expectations clearly - Tankan Survey (Large Manufacturers Index + Large Non-Manufacturers Index, latest quarter): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - Japan GDP (latest quarterly print): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - Citigroup Economic Surprise Index for USD and JPY (exact values — positive = beating expectations; state "Data Unavailable" if not found) - Any high-impact US or Japan data releases scheduled TODAY that are a risk for USDJPY 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - US Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 confirmed data drivers - Japan Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 confirmed data drivers - Shunto Wage Growth: [X% or "Not yet released this cycle"] | BOJ Impact: [Hawkish / Neutral / Insufficient for hike] - Citi USD Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi JPY Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Fundamental Upper Hand: [USD / JPY / Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - ECONOMIC MOMENTUM (US vs Japan)] - US Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - Japan Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - Shunto Wage Growth: [% or Not Released] | BOJ Rate Hike Implication: [Hawkish / Neutral / Bearish for hike] - Tokyo CPI Core: [Actual vs Forecast] | Tankan Large Manufacturers: [Actual vs Forecast] - Citi USD Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi JPY Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [which economy is outperforming, whether Shunto supports a BOJ hike, and what it means for USDJPY] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase4:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest USDJPY positioning and sentiment data. Fetch and report: - CFTC COT report: NET Non-Commercial (speculative) Japanese Yen futures contracts — exact number, long or short. Note: Speculators are historically and structurally net SHORT JPY (long USDJPY). Is the current net short JPY position at a historical extreme? If specs are excessively short JPY, this raises mean-reversion / short-squeeze risk for a sharp USDJPY drop. Use this data strictly for identifying the overarching macro trend context and potential crowded-trade risk. Do NOT use COT for short-term intraday signals. - IG Client Sentiment: exact % of retail traders Long vs Short USDJPY right now. Weight this data higher for short-term and intraday contrarian setups — when retail is heavily skewed one way (e.g., 70%+ long USDJPY), apply a contrarian bearish lean for today's intraday bias. State clearly: is a contrarian intraday opportunity present? - VIX level and S&P 500 direction — CRITICAL for USDJPY: JPY is a safe-haven currency. VIX spiking (risk-off) typically drives JPY strength (USDJPY down). VIX falling (risk-on) typically drives JPY weakness (USDJPY up). State the risk-on/risk-off implication explicitly. - CNN Fear & Greed Index — current level and reading - Is retail sentiment at a contrarian extreme? 📊 FX OPTIONS EXPIRIES CHECK — USDJPY (MANDATORY FOR FOREX SENTIMENT): Browse for any large FX options expiries or option barrier cuts scheduled for TODAY [CURRENT_DATE] at key strike prices for USDJPY. The two primary cut times are: (1) Tokyo Cut: 02:00 UTC — primarily used for Asian-session options. (2) New York Cut (NY Cut): 15:00 UTC — the dominant global cut time, where the largest notional options expire. SOURCES: Check DTCC swap data repository, Reuters IFR Markets options commentary, CME Group FX options data, or Bloomberg FX options scanner. LOGIC: — Large open interest at a key strike acts as a PRICE MAGNET in the hours leading up to the cut — dealers hedge their gamma exposure by pushing price toward the strike. — Once a large strike expires, the 'gravitational pull' disappears and price can move freely — this is often a source of sharp post-cut volatility. — A large option BARRIER (knock-in or knock-out) at a key level can make price action 'sticky' near that barrier — it acts as a WALL rather than a magnet. If no significant expiries are found, state: 'No significant USDJPY options expiries noted for today.' ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate strikes or notional sizes. If you cannot find confirmed data, state 'Options expiry data unavailable — check IFR Markets or CME for confirmation.' Output additions: - USDJPY NY Cut Options (15:00 UTC): [strike level + notional size in USD/EUR millions, or 'No significant expiry'] (Source: [domain]) | Effect: [Price Magnet at [level] / Barrier Wall at [level] / No notable effect] - USDJPY Tokyo Cut Options (02:00 UTC): [strike level + notional, or 'No significant expiry'] | Effect: [Magnet / Wall / None] - Options Expiry Directional Bias Implication: [Bullish USDJPY — magnet above / Bearish USDJPY — magnet below / Neutral — no dominant expiry] Add to compressed summary block: - Options Expiries (USDJPY): [NY Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | [Tokyo Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | Bias Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - CFTC Net JPY: [contracts + long/short] | Extreme Short JPY? [Yes/No] | Short-Squeeze Risk: [High/Medium/Low] | Macro Trend Context: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral USDJPY macro positioning] - IG Retail USDJPY: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - VIX: [level] | S&P 500: [direction] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On → JPY weakness / Risk-Off → JPY strength] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Overall Sentiment Bias: [Bullish USDJPY / Bearish USDJPY / Contrarian] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P4 - SENTIMENT & POSITIONING] - CFTC Net JPY Futures: [contracts + long/short + extreme? Yes/No] | Short-Squeeze Risk: [High/Medium/Low] | Macro Context: [trend direction for USDJPY] - IG Retail Sentiment: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - VIX: [level] | S&P 500: [direction] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On (bearish JPY) / Risk-Off (bullish JPY)] - Core Logic: [contrarian intraday signal from IG retail + crowded-trade risk from COT + VIX risk mode — which edge applies today for USDJPY] - Bias: [Bullish USDJPY / Bearish USDJPY / Contrarian] ```

phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear USDJPY institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Policy Divergence (Fed vs BOJ): [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Yield Differentials (US Treasury vs JGB): [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Economic Momentum (US vs Japan): [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment & Positioning (COT JPY + IG): [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Macro Risk & Energy Dynamics: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. USDJPY is particularly sensitive to contradictions between the yield spread signal (P2) and safe-haven/risk flows (P4/P5) — if 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- Pay special attention to: (a) MoF intervention risk if USDJPY is elevated (P1), (b) whether the JPY COT short is crowded enough to create short-squeeze risk (P4), and (c) whether oil prices are amplifying or dampening the fundamental bias (P5).
- If all factors align (e.g., wide US-JP yield spread + risk-on + weak BOJ + strong US data), confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity. Note: The Tokyo session is important for USDJPY due to BOJ-related news; the NY session drives the largest price moves via US data releases.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
